back_translation: O LORD, why do You stand far off? Why do You hide Yourself in times of trouble?
In pride the wicked hotly pursue the needy; let them be caught in the schemes they have devised.
For the wicked boasts in the cravings of his heart; he blesses the greedy and reviles the LORD.
In his pride the wicked does not seek God; in all his thoughts there is no God.
His ways prosper at all times; Your lofty judgments are far beyond his sight; he sneers at all his foes.
He says to himself, 'I will not be moved; through all generations I will meet no adversity.'
His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and oppression; under his tongue are trouble and evil.
He lies in wait near the villages; he kills the innocent in secret; his eyes watch for the helpless.
He lies in wait in secret like a lion in its lair; he lurks to seize the poor, and seizes the poor by dragging him into his net.
The victims are crushed and fall down; the helpless fall into his mighty grip.
He says to himself, 'God has forgotten; He no longer sees; He has hidden His face.'
O LORD, arise! O God, do not forget the oppressed; lift up Your hand.
Why does the wicked revile God, and say to himself, 'You will not call me to account'?
But You see; You observe trouble and grief; the helpless commits himself to You; You are the helper of the fatherless, to repay it with Your hand.
Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer; call him to account for his wickedness until none remains.
The LORD is King forever and ever; the nations will perish from His land.
O LORD, You hear the desire of the humble; You will strengthen their hearts, and You will incline Your ear,
to bring justice to the fatherless and the oppressed, so that man from the earth may strike terror no more.
